concepts.label: Cell
concepts.definition: The basic structural and functional unit of all living organisms

concepts.label: DNA
concepts.definition: Deoxyribonucleic acid, the molecule that contains genetic information

concepts.label: RNA
concepts.definition: Ribonucleic acid, involved in protein synthesis and gene regulation

concepts.label: Protein
concepts.definition: Large molecules composed of amino acids that perform many cellular functions

concepts.label: Gene
concepts.definition: A distinct sequence of nucleotides that codes for a specific trait

concepts.label: Chromosome
concepts.definition: A structure containing DNA and associated proteins found in the nucleus

concepts.label: Mutation
concepts.definition: A change in the DNA sequence that can alter gene function

concepts.label: Natural Selection
concepts.definition: The process by which organisms with favorable traits survive and reproduce more successfully

concepts.label: Evolution
concepts.definition: The change in heritable traits of biological populations over successive generations

concepts.label: Ecosystem
concepts.definition: A community of living organisms interacting with their physical environment

concepts.label: Organism
concepts.definition: An individual living system capable of functioning independently

concepts.label: Species
concepts.definition: A group of organisms capable of interbreeding and producing fertile offspring

concepts.label: Photosynthesis
concepts.definition: Process by which plants convert light energy into chemical energy

concepts.label: Cellular Respiration
concepts.definition: Process by which cells break down glucose to produce ATP energy

concepts.label: Mitosis
concepts.definition: Cell division process that produces two identical diploid cells

concepts.label: Meiosis
concepts.definition: Cell division process that produces four genetically different gametes

concepts.label: Gamete
concepts.definition: A reproductive cell (sperm or egg) containing half the genetic material

concepts.label: Fertilization
concepts.definition: The fusion of male and female gametes to form a zygote

concepts.label: Zygote
concepts.definition: A fertilized egg cell that develops into an organism

concepts.label: Embryo
concepts.definition: An organism in its early stages of development

concepts.label: Nucleus
concepts.definition: The membrane-bound organelle containing the cell's genetic material

concepts.label: Mitochondria
concepts.definition: Organelles that produce ATP through cellular respiration

concepts.label: Chloroplast
concepts.definition: Organelles in plant cells where photosynthesis occurs

concepts.label: Ribosome
concepts.definition: Cellular structures that synthesize proteins

concepts.label: Endoplasmic Reticulum
concepts.definition: A network of membranes involved in protein and lipid synthesis

concepts.label: Golgi Apparatus
concepts.definition: Organelle that modifies, packages, and ships proteins

concepts.label: Cell Membrane
concepts.definition: The phospholipid bilayer that controls what enters and exits the cell

concepts.label: Cell Wall
concepts.definition: A rigid structure surrounding plant cells that provides support

concepts.label: Cytoplasm
concepts.definition: The gel-like substance inside cells where organelles are suspended

concepts.label: Enzyme
concepts.definition: A protein that catalyzes biochemical reactions by lowering activation energy

concepts.label: ATP
concepts.definition: Adenosine triphosphate, the primary energy currency of cells

concepts.label: Amino Acid
concepts.definition: Building blocks of proteins containing amino and carboxyl groups

concepts.label: Nucleotide
concepts.definition: Building blocks of DNA and RNA consisting of a base, sugar, and phosphate

concepts.label: Base Pair
concepts.definition: Complementary nucleotides that form hydrogen bonds in DNA (A-T, G-C)

concepts.label: Transcription
concepts.definition: The process of copying DNA into RNA

concepts.label: Translation
concepts.definition: The process of converting RNA into proteins

concepts.label: Codon
concepts.definition: A sequence of three nucleotides that codes for a specific amino acid

concepts.label: Anticodon
concepts.definition: A sequence of three nucleotides in tRNA complementary to a codon

concepts.label: mRNA
concepts.definition: Messenger RNA that carries genetic information from DNA to ribosomes

concepts.label: tRNA
concepts.definition: Transfer RNA that brings amino acids to the ribosome during translation

concepts.label: rRNA
concepts.definition: Ribosomal RNA that forms the structural and catalytic components of ribosomes

concepts.label: Allele
concepts.definition: Different versions of the same gene

concepts.label: Dominant Allele
concepts.definition: An allele that is expressed when present in one or two copies

concepts.label: Recessive Allele
concepts.definition: An allele that is only expressed when present in two copies

concepts.label: Genotype
concepts.definition: The genetic makeup of an organism

concepts.label: Phenotype
concepts.definition: The observable characteristics of an organism

concepts.label: Homozygous
concepts.definition: Having two identical alleles for a particular gene

concepts.label: Heterozygous
concepts.definition: Having two different alleles for a particular gene

concepts.label: Heredity
concepts.definition: The passing of traits from parents to offspring

concepts.label: Mendel's Laws
concepts.definition: Principles of inheritance discovered by Gregor Mendel

concepts.label: Independent Assortment
concepts.definition: Genes for different traits are inherited independently

concepts.label: Segregation
concepts.definition: The separation of allele pairs during gamete formation

concepts.label: Genetic Drift
concepts.definition: Random changes in allele frequencies in small populations

concepts.label: Gene Flow
concepts.definition: The transfer of genetic material between populations

concepts.label: Hardy-Weinberg Equilibrium
concepts.definition: A principle stating that allele frequencies remain constant in an ideal population

concepts.label: Adaptation
concepts.definition: A trait that increases an organism's fitness in its environment

concepts.label: Fitness
concepts.definition: An organism's ability to survive and reproduce in its environment

concepts.label: Speciation
concepts.definition: The formation of new species through evolutionary processes

concepts.label: Reproductive Isolation
concepts.definition: Barriers that prevent species from interbreeding

concepts.label: Homology
concepts.definition: Similarity in structure due to shared evolutionary origin

concepts.label: Analogy
concepts.definition: Similarity in function but not structure due to convergent evolution

concepts.label: Vestigial Structure
concepts.definition: A structure that has lost most or all of its ancestral function

concepts.label: Fossil Record
concepts.definition: The history of life preserved in rock layers

concepts.label: Taxonomy
concepts.definition: The science of classifying and naming organisms

concepts.label: Phylogeny
concepts.definition: The evolutionary relationships among species

concepts.label: Domain
concepts.definition: The highest taxonomic category dividing all life

concepts.label: Kingdom
concepts.definition: A major taxonomic group within a domain

concepts.label: Phylum
concepts.definition: A taxonomic group of related classes

concepts.label: Class
concepts.definition: A taxonomic group of related orders

concepts.label: Order
concepts.definition: A taxonomic group of related families

concepts.label: Family
concepts.definition: A taxonomic group of related genera

concepts.label: Genus
concepts.definition: A taxonomic group of closely related species

concepts.label: Prokaryote
concepts.definition: Organisms lacking a membrane-bound nucleus

concepts.label: Eukaryote
concepts.definition: Organisms with a membrane-bound nucleus

concepts.label: Bacteria
concepts.definition: Single-celled prokaryotic organisms

concepts.label: Archaea
concepts.definition: Single-celled prokaryotic organisms distinct from bacteria

concepts.label: Virus
concepts.definition: Non-cellular infectious agents that require host cells to reproduce

concepts.label: Fungi
concepts.definition: Eukaryotic organisms that obtain nutrients through absorption

concepts.label: Plant
concepts.definition: Multicellular eukaryotic organisms that perform photosynthesis

concepts.label: Animal
concepts.definition: Multicellular eukaryotic organisms that consume other organisms for energy

concepts.label: Protist
concepts.definition: Diverse group of eukaryotic organisms, mostly single-celled

concepts.label: Autotroph
concepts.definition: An organism that produces its own food using inorganic compounds

concepts.label: Heterotroph
concepts.definition: An organism that obtains nutrients by consuming other organisms

concepts.label: Producer
concepts.definition: An organism that makes its own food through photosynthesis or chemosynthesis

concepts.label: Primary Consumer
concepts.definition: An organism that feeds directly on producers

concepts.label: Secondary Consumer
concepts.definition: An organism that feeds on primary consumers

concepts.label: Decomposer
concepts.definition: An organism that breaks down dead organic matter

concepts.label: Food Chain
concepts.definition: A linear sequence of organisms showing who eats whom

concepts.label: Food Web
concepts.definition: A complex network of interconnected food chains

concepts.label: Energy Pyramid
concepts.definition: A diagram showing the flow of energy through trophic levels

concepts.label: Trophic Level
concepts.definition: The position an organism occupies in a food chain

concepts.label: Biomass
concepts.definition: The total mass of living organisms in a given area

concepts.label: Population
concepts.definition: A group of organisms of the same species living in the same area

concepts.label: Community
concepts.definition: All the populations of different species living in the same area

concepts.label: Biome
concepts.definition: A large ecological area with distinctive plant and animal communities

concepts.label: Habitat
concepts.definition: The natural environment where an organism lives and meets its needs

concepts.label: Ecological Niche
concepts.definition: The role an organism plays in its ecosystem, including its habitat and interactions

concepts.label: Competition
concepts.definition: The struggle between organisms for limited resources

concepts.label: Predation
concepts.definition: The consumption of one organism by another

concepts.label: Symbiosis
concepts.definition: A close ecological relationship between two different species

concepts.label: Mutualism
concepts.definition: A symbiotic relationship where both species benefit

concepts.label: Commensalism
concepts.definition: A symbiotic relationship where one species benefits and the other is unaffected

concepts.label: Parasitism
concepts.definition: A symbiotic relationship where one species benefits at the expense of the other

concepts.label: Carbon Cycle
concepts.definition: The movement of carbon through ecosystems

concepts.label: Nitrogen Cycle
concepts.definition: The movement of nitrogen through ecosystems

concepts.label: Water Cycle
concepts.definition: The continuous movement of water through the environment

concepts.label: Phosphorus Cycle
concepts.definition: The movement of phosphorus through ecosystems

concepts.label: Carrying Capacity
concepts.definition: The maximum population size an environment can sustain

concepts.label: Limiting Factor
concepts.definition: A resource or condition that restricts population growth

concepts.label: Exponential Growth
concepts.definition: Rapid population growth under ideal conditions

concepts.label: Logistic Growth
concepts.definition: Population growth that levels off as it approaches carrying capacity

concepts.label: Biodiversity
concepts.definition: The variety of life in an ecosystem or on Earth

concepts.label: Extinction
concepts.definition: The complete disappearance of a species from Earth

concepts.label: Conservation
concepts.definition: The protection and preservation of natural resources and species

concepts.label: Succession
concepts.definition: The gradual change in species composition over time

concepts.label: Primary Succession
concepts.definition: Succession that occurs on previously lifeless surfaces

concepts.label: Secondary Succession
concepts.definition: Succession that occurs after a disturbance in an existing ecosystem

concepts.label: Climax Community
concepts.definition: A stable, mature ecological community at the end of succession

concepts.label: Homeostasis
concepts.definition: The maintenance of stable internal conditions in living organisms

concepts.label: Feedback Loop
concepts.definition: A biological mechanism that regulates physiological processes

concepts.label: Negative Feedback
concepts.definition: A regulatory mechanism that counteracts changes to maintain stability

concepts.label: Positive Feedback
concepts.definition: A regulatory mechanism that amplifies changes

concepts.label: Nervous System
concepts.definition: The network of nerve cells that controls body functions

concepts.label: Neuron
concepts.definition: A nerve cell that transmits electrical and chemical signals

concepts.label: Synapse
concepts.definition: The junction between two neurons where signals are transmitted

concepts.label: Neurotransmitter
concepts.definition: Chemical messengers that transmit signals across synapses

concepts.label: Central Nervous System
concepts.definition: The brain and spinal cord that process and coordinate information

concepts.label: Peripheral Nervous System
concepts.definition: All nerves outside the brain and spinal cord

concepts.label: Reflex
concepts.definition: An automatic response to a stimulus that bypasses conscious thought

concepts.label: Hormone
concepts.definition: Chemical messengers produced by glands that regulate body functions

concepts.label: Endocrine System
concepts.definition: The system of glands that produce and secrete hormones

concepts.label: Circulatory System
concepts.definition: The system that transports blood, nutrients, and gases throughout the body

concepts.label: Heart
concepts.definition: The muscular organ that pumps blood through the circulatory system

concepts.label: Blood
concepts.definition: The fluid that carries oxygen, nutrients, and waste products through the body

concepts.label: Red Blood Cell
concepts.definition: Blood cells that carry oxygen from lungs to body tissues

concepts.label: White Blood Cell
concepts.definition: Blood cells that defend the body against infections and diseases

concepts.label: Platelet
concepts.definition: Blood cell fragments that help with blood clotting

concepts.label: Respiratory System
concepts.definition: The system that exchanges oxygen and carbon dioxide with the environment

concepts.label: Lung
concepts.definition: The organ where gas exchange occurs between air and blood

concepts.label: Alveolus
concepts.definition: Tiny air sacs in the lungs where gas exchange occurs

concepts.label: Digestive System
concepts.definition: The system that breaks down food and absorbs nutrients

concepts.label: Stomach
concepts.definition: The organ that chemically and mechanically breaks down food

concepts.label: Small Intestine
concepts.definition: The organ where most nutrient absorption occurs

concepts.label: Immune System
concepts.definition: The body's defense system against pathogens and foreign substances

concepts.label: Antibody
concepts.definition: Proteins produced by immune cells that recognize and neutralize antigens

concepts.label: Antigen
concepts.definition: Foreign substances that trigger an immune response

concepts.label: Vaccine
concepts.definition: A preparation that stimulates immunity against a specific disease

concepts.label: Tissue
concepts.definition: A group of similar cells that work together to perform a specific function

concepts.label: Organ
concepts.definition: A structure composed of different tissues that work together

concepts.label: Organ System
concepts.definition: A group of organs that work together to perform major functions

concepts.label: Multicellularity
concepts.definition: The condition of being composed of many cells

concepts.label: Cell Differentiation
concepts.definition: The process by which cells become specialized for specific functions

concepts.label: Stem Cell
concepts.definition: Unspecialized cells that can develop into various cell types

concepts.label: Development
concepts.definition: The process of growth and differentiation from embryo to adult

concepts.label: Regeneration
concepts.definition: The ability to regrow lost or damaged body parts

concepts.label: Aging
concepts.definition: The gradual deterioration of physiological functions over time

concepts.label: Apoptosis
concepts.definition: Programmed cell death that occurs as a normal part of development

concepts.label: Cancer
concepts.definition: Uncontrolled cell division that can spread to other parts of the body

concepts.label: Tumor
concepts.definition: An abnormal growth of cells that can be benign or malignant

concepts.label: Metastasis
concepts.definition: The spread of cancer cells from the original site to other parts of the body

concepts.label: Biotechnology
concepts.definition: The use of living organisms or their products to benefit humans

concepts.label: Genetic Engineering
concepts.definition: The direct manipulation of genes to alter organisms

concepts.label: Recombinant DNA
concepts.definition: DNA formed by combining genetic material from different sources

concepts.label: PCR
concepts.definition: Polymerase Chain Reaction, a technique for amplifying DNA sequences

concepts.label: Gel Electrophoresis
concepts.definition: A technique for separating DNA, RNA, or proteins by size

concepts.label: Cloning
concepts.definition: The process of creating genetically identical organisms

concepts.label: Gene Therapy
concepts.definition: The introduction of genes into cells to treat disease

concepts.label: GMO
concepts.definition: Genetically Modified Organism whose DNA has been altered using biotechnology

concepts.label: Bioinformatics
concepts.definition: The application of computer science to biological data analysis

concepts.label: Genomics
concepts.definition: The study of an organism's complete set of genetic material

concepts.label: Proteomics
concepts.definition: The study of the complete set of proteins produced by an organism
